title: Workers can be created without linked tickets
summary: The worker store allows creation with an empty `ticketIds` list, violating the spec requirement that every worker link to at least one ticket and weakening ticket-led execution truth.
recommendedAction: Require at least one linked ticket during worker creation and reject ticketless workers from the command/tool surface.

title: Consolidation bypasses approval gate
summary: `recordConsolidation` accepts successful outcomes even when the worker was never approved for consolidation, allowing worker state to jump to completed without a manager approval decision.
recommendedAction: Enforce `approved_for_consolidation` as a precondition for successful consolidation outcomes and reject or error otherwise.

title: Prepared launches are persisted as active work
summary: Preparing a launch flips workers to active and increments launch metadata before a subprocess actually starts, leaving durable state inconsistent with runtime truth.
recommendedAction: Separate launch preparation from active execution state; only transition to active once a subprocess actually starts or explicitly record a distinct prepared state.

title: Worker execution results are not persisted back into launch state
summary: After running a worker subprocess, the tool returns the result but does not update durable launch status or worker state, making recovery and audit untruthful.
recommendedAction: Persist execution completion/failure/cancellation back into worker launch state and related worker telemetry/status before returning.

title: Retirement cleanup trusts arbitrary workspace paths too broadly
summary: Retirement may remove an arbitrary existing directory if a stale or tampered runtime descriptor points outside the managed worker runtime root and `git worktree remove` fails.
recommendedAction: Constrain retirement cleanup to managed worker runtime paths rooted under `.loom/runtime/workers` and refuse destructive cleanup outside that boundary.

title: Governance documents still omit the worker layer in key enumerations
summary: Constitutional and AGENTS documentation still describe the stack and command family without workers in several places, leaving the implemented architecture partially undocumented.
recommendedAction: Update AGENTS and constitutional docs to include the worker layer and `/worker` command family where layer enumerations and flow descriptions are now stale.

title: Workers can be created without linked tickets
summary: The worker store allows creation with an empty `ticketIds` list, violating the spec requirement that every worker link to at least one ticket and weakening ticket-led execution truth.
recommendedAction: Require at least one linked ticket during worker creation and reject ticketless workers from the command/tool surface.
resolutionNotes: `createWorker` now normalizes linked refs first and throws when `ticketIds` is empty. Command/runtime tests were updated to supply linked tickets explicitly, and full workspace lint/typecheck/test passed.

title: Consolidation bypasses approval gate
summary: `recordConsolidation` accepts successful outcomes even when the worker was never approved for consolidation, allowing worker state to jump to completed without a manager approval decision.
recommendedAction: Enforce `approved_for_consolidation` as a precondition for successful consolidation outcomes and reject or error otherwise.
resolutionNotes: `recordConsolidation` now rejects all consolidation outcomes unless the worker is already in `approved_for_consolidation`. Tests now cover both pre-approval rejection and post-approval deferred consolidation.

title: Prepared launches are persisted as active work
summary: Preparing a launch flips workers to active and increments launch metadata before a subprocess actually starts, leaving durable state inconsistent with runtime truth.
recommendedAction: Separate launch preparation from active execution state; only transition to active once a subprocess actually starts or explicitly record a distinct prepared state.
resolutionNotes: Launch preparation now remains truthful: prepare-only leaves worker status/telemetry unchanged, while `startLaunchExecution` performs the active transition only when execution actually begins. Tests assert prepare-only behavior.

title: Worker execution results are not persisted back into launch state
summary: After running a worker subprocess, the tool returns the result but does not update durable launch status or worker state, making recovery and audit untruthful.
recommendedAction: Persist execution completion/failure/cancellation back into worker launch state and related worker telemetry/status before returning.
resolutionNotes: `worker_launch`/`worker_resume` now call `startLaunchExecution` and `finishLaunchExecution`, which persist running and final execution state into durable worker/launch records. Tool tests cover persisted launch outcomes.

title: Retirement cleanup trusts arbitrary workspace paths too broadly
summary: Retirement may remove an arbitrary existing directory if a stale or tampered runtime descriptor points outside the managed worker runtime root and `git worktree remove` fails.
recommendedAction: Constrain retirement cleanup to managed worker runtime paths rooted under `.loom/runtime/workers` and refuse destructive cleanup outside that boundary.
resolutionNotes: Runtime cleanup now resolves managed runtime paths relative to `.loom/runtime/workers` and throws on unmanaged paths rather than deleting them. Store tests cover the refusal path.

title: Governance documents still omit the worker layer in key enumerations
summary: Constitutional and AGENTS documentation still describe the stack and command family without workers in several places, leaving the implemented architecture partially undocumented.
recommendedAction: Update AGENTS and constitutional docs to include the worker layer and `/worker` command family where layer enumerations and flow descriptions are now stale.
resolutionNotes: AGENTS and the cited constitutional artifacts were updated to include workers in layer/graph enumerations and `/worker + worker_*` in command-family guidance while preserving bounded ticket/Ralph semantics.
